de-h-armee-17.notes: Identity scaffold for the Eastern Front sector simulation; positions derive from authored army sectors (no divisional OOB yet — SCALE_PLAN S2).

positions.label: Case Blue begins east of Kharkov

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014

de-h-armee-6.notes: Pilot coverage: Case Blue to the surrender. Pre-1942 positions not yet authored. The reformed 6. Armee (Mar 1943) is a separate incarnation, not yet authored.

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014
Niehorster, L. — German 6th Army order of battle, 19 Nov 1942

de-h-jgd-100.notes: Pilot: corps assignment held constant over the pilot window; positions derived from the corps sector (approximate).

positions.label: Crossed the Volga into the burning city (Rodimtsev)

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014

su-gd-rd-13.notes: Converted from the 87th Rifle Division. Famous for the 14–15 Sep crossing and the central landing stage. Pilot: city positions schematic (west-bank strip); the division held a narrow riverside sector inside the drawn pocket ring, so side checks report it as contested.

positions.label: Between Mamayev Kurgan and the factories (Gorishny)

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014

su-rd-95.notes: Second formation. Later 75th Guards. Pilot: city positions schematic (west-bank strip); the division held a narrow riverside sector inside the drawn pocket ring, so side checks report it as contested.
